title: Enhancement of superconducting stiffness in hybrid superconducting-metallic bilayers
authors: J. E. Ebot, Lorenzo Pizzino, Sam Mardazad, Johannes S. Hofmann, Thierry Giamarchi, Adrian Kantian
abstract: Boosting superconductivity by metallic reservoirs is the essence of Kivelson's bilayer proposal.   One layer provides pairing to the electrons, while the weakly coupled metal provides additional phase coherence to those pairs by mediating extended-range pair-pair coupling.   Demonstrating significant and unambiguous performance gains with strong-coupling methods for such set-ups had been difficult.   In the present work, we study these systems doped away from half-filling, corresponding to a partially spin-polarized 1D Anderson- or Kondo-lattice.   We show that this breaks the coexistence of dominant superconducting and density-density correlations decisively in favour or the former.   Consequently, we provide evidence that in this doped regime, superconducting near-long-range order is not precluded by a small charge-gap in the thermodynamic limit, as we have recently shown to be the case at half-filling [JE Ebot $et$ $al.$, arXiv:2602.11153 [cond-mat.supr-con]].   We study the complex manner in which the enhancement of superconductivity in the pairing layer depends on the parameters of the metal, and especially that both pairing-limited and stiffness-limited regimes may appear in these systems.   In addition to superconducting bilayers, our results are relevant, via a particle-hole transformation, for heavy-fermion Kondo-lattice materials in magnetic fields, as we provide previously lacking insight on the competition between antiferromagnetic and easy-plane magnetism, as well as a route for comprehensive indirect tests of Kivelson's bilayer proposal well beyond previous capabilities.
summary: This paper demonstrates that doping a hybrid superconducting-metallic bilayer away from half-filling enhances superconducting correlations by breaking the competition with density-density correlations. It identifies distinct regimes of superconductivity controlled by the metal's parameters and provides a mapping to heavy-fermion systems. This work offers a new way to study Kivelson's bilayer proposal using magnetic materials.
main_problem: The study investigates how a metallic reservoir can enhance superconducting correlations in a hybrid bilayer system when doped away from half-filling, specifically addressing whether the charge gap present at half-filling suppresses superconductivity.
main_result: Doping the system away from half-filling breaks the degeneracy between superconducting and density-density correlations in favor of superconductivity, allowing for near-long-range order and revealing both stiffness-limited and amplitude-limited regimes.
method: The authors utilize large-scale numerical simulations, including Density Matrix Renimormalization Group (DMRG) and Auxiliary Field Quantum Monte Carlo (AFQMC), alongside analytical frameworks like Conformal Field Theory.
model_or_system: A 1D hybrid bilayer model consisting of a pairing subsystem (P-subsystem) with on-site pairing and a metallic subsystem (M-subsystem) coupled via inter-layer tunneling, which can be mapped to the periodic Anderson or Kondo lattice models.
key_observables: S-wave pair-pair correlations, density-density correlations, single-particle correlations, Luttinger liquid parameters (Kp and Km), and superconducting susceptibility.
important_parameters: Interaction strength (U), inter-layer tunneling (t_perp), metallic kinetic energy (t_m), filling fraction (n), and density imbalance between layers.
main_assumptions: The study focuses on 1D chains; the extension of these findings to 2D and 3D systems remains a theoretical prediction.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the physical setup of the two weakly coupled chains (P-subsystem and M-subsystem) used in the model.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Kivelson bilayer proposal, defines the 1D hybrid model and its mapping to magnetic lattices, presents numerical results for different doping and coupling regimes, discusses the transition between amplitude and stiffness-limited superconductivity, and concludes with implications for heavy-fermion materials.
why_interesting: The paper provides a theoretical bridge between superconductivity enhancement and magnetic ordering in Kondo/Anderson lattices, offering a way to use spin-polarized heavy-fermion materials as simulators for superconducting bilayer physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematic representation of the Hamiltonian in Eq. (1). It comprises of a pairing subsystem (“P-subsystem”) with on-site pairing U (and chemical potential µm, when performing AFQMC simulations, which are in the grand- canonical regime), and a metallic subsystem (M-subsystem; we will use ‘metal’ interchangeably) with nearest-neighbour tunneling tm and chemical potential term µm. Coupling be- tween the subsystems is provided by tunneling with amplitude t⊥.
